trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Canada exports wheat, machinery, aircraft, and vehicles to South Africa. South Africa exports platinum group metals, gold, diamonds, coal, and vehicles to Canada. South Africa is a key source of platinum group metals important for Canadian automotive and clean energy industries.

diplomatic.summary: Generally positive relations between two Commonwealth members with shared historical democratic transitions. Canada supported South Africa's anti-apartheid struggle and Nelson Mandela. Both participate in Commonwealth Heads of Government meetings. Canada has human rights concerns about some South African governance issues.

military.summary: Limited military cooperation through Commonwealth frameworks. Both participate in UN peacekeeping. Some training exchanges. No formal alliance.

financial.summary: Canadian mining companies are major investors in South Africa (Barrick, Agnico-Eagle adjacent through gold sector). South African mining conglomerates (Anglo American, De Beers through its South African roots) have Canadian market connections. Growing interest in critical minerals.

energy.summary: South Africa exports coal to Canada (minor quantities). Growing interest in South Africa's renewable energy potential for Canadian investment. Cooperation on nuclear energy technology (CANDU relevance).

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in astronomy (South Africa hosts MeerKAT/SKA), environmental science, and health. Commonwealth academic exchanges support researcher mobility. Joint programs in mining technology and geological sciences.
